data.q: Which PHP standard recommends PSR-1 and PSR-2 for basic coding style and code style guide?
data.e: PSR-1 and PSR-2 define basic coding standards and a coding style guide for PHP code to improve readability and interoperability.

data.q: What is the primary focus of PSR-12 in PHP?
data.e: PSR-12 extends PSR-2 and provides an extended and more detailed PHP coding style guide.

data.q: According to modern PHP coding standards, which declaration style is preferred for visibility in properties and methods?
o: Always explicitly declare visibility as public, protected, or private
data.e: Explicitly declaring visibility improves code clarity and is recommended in modern PHP coding standards.

data.q: In PSR-1 and PSR-2 standards, how should opening curly braces be placed for classes and methods?
o: On a separate line with one tab indent
data.e: Opening braces for classes and methods MUST go on the next line, not the same line as the declaration.

data.q: What does PSR-4 define in PHP code quality standards?
data.e: PSR-4 specifies how to autoload classes from file paths. It replaces the older PSR-0 standard.

data.q: Which of the following is recommended by PHP coding standards for naming classes?
data.e: Class names SHOULD be declared in PascalCase according to PSR coding standards.

data.q: What is the recommended line length limit in PHP PSR-2 coding style guide?
data.e: PSR-2 recommends lines SHOULD not exceed 120 characters; 80 is a widely adopted legacy convention.

data.q: In modern PHP coding standards, how should files be named?
o: Class name matching the file name in PascalCase
data.e: A file SHOULD declare strict types, contain only one class, and that class name should match the file name.

data.q: Which tool is commonly used to automatically fix PHP coding style issues according to PSR standards?
data.e: PHP_CodeSniffer (phpcs) detects coding standard violations; phpcbf can automatically fix many of those issues.

data.q: Which of the following is a key benefits of following PHP coding standards like PSR-12?
data.e: Coding standards primarily enhance readability, maintainability, and consistency across codebases.
